description_long: This object is a handheld electric drill with a cylindrical, ribbed grip section and a prominent chuck at the top for holding drill bits. The body is primarily black with metallic and possibly plastic components, and features a visible coiled section that may represent a vent or heat dissipation area. On one side, there is a rectangular control panel with colored buttons or indicators, likely for operation or speed adjustment. The bottom end is slightly tapered and may include a loop or hook for hanging or storage. The overall design is robust, with a mix of matte and glossy finishes, and the object appears to be constructed for durability and frequent use. No visible power cord or battery pack is attached, and there are no clear brand markings.
description: A robust, black electric drill with a ribbed grip, metallic chuck, and a side control panel with colored buttons. The design is durable and functional, with a coiled vent section and a loop at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ribbed grip, coiled vent section, and the metallic chuck at the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rectangular control panel with colored buttons and the loop at the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the ribbed grip and the plain back side without visible controls.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side profile of the control panel and the overall cylindrical shape.
